subject: REL
course_description: Explores the rituals and practices of Judaism, placing them in their historical context and examining the theological concepts that underpin them.
course_notes: (Same as JS 225 1) This course satisfies the Religion major requirement:- Tradition-Judaism. Judaism is the religion of the Jewish people, the religious core of Jewish civilization. This course provides a survey introduction to Judaism from the perspective of the academic, university-based, study of religion. Asking what makes Judaism an example of "religion," and comparing it to other traditions, especially Christianity and Islam, we will explore Jewish beliefs and practices with special attention to the concept of the Jewish people's special relationship with God ("chosenness") and with a land (Israel), forms and content of prayer, and foodways (dietary laws and customs). The course will welcome students of all backgrounds and identities. Prior knowledge of Judaism will not be assumed or required.
